The number of entries for the [figure skating events](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics "Figure skating at the 2022 Winter Olympics") at the Winter Olympics is determined by quotas set by the [International Olympic Committee](/wiki/International_Olympic_Committee "International Olympic Committee"). A total of 144 quota spots were available to athletes to compete in the figure skating events at the [2022 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2022_Winter_Olympics "2022 Winter Olympics"). There were 30 spots allotted each in [men's and women's singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), 19 in [pair skating](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating"), and 23 in [ice dance](/wiki/Ice_dance "Ice dance"). Additionally, ten nations qualified for the [team event](/wiki/List_of_Olympic_medalists_in_figure_skating#Team_event "List of Olympic medalists in figure skating"). There is no individual athlete qualification to the Olympics; the choice of whom to send is at the discretion of each country's [National Olympic Committee](/wiki/National_Olympic_Committee "National Olympic Committee"). Each National Olympic Committee could enter up to 18 skaters total, with a maximum of nine men or nine women.

## Qualification of nations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Figure_skating_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Qualification&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Qualification of nations")]

Countries were able to qualify entries to the [2022 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2022_Winter_Olympics "2022 Winter Olympics") in two ways. Most spots were allocated based on the results of the [2021 World Figure Skating Championships](/wiki/2021_World_Figure_Skating_Championships "2021 World Figure Skating Championships"). There, countries were able to qualify up to three entries in each discipline according to a predetermined system.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The results of the 2021 World Championships determined 83 total spots: 24 entries each in men's and women's singles, 16 in pairs, and 19 in ice dance.[[2]](#cite_note-ISU-2388-2)

The remainder of the spots were filled at the [2021 Nebelhorn Trophy](/wiki/2021_CS_Nebelhorn_Trophy "2021 CS Nebelhorn Trophy") in late September 2021. Countries that had already earned an entry to the Olympics were not allowed to qualify additional entries at this final qualifying competition. However, if a country earned two or three spots at the World Championships, but did not have two or three skaters, respectively, qualify for the free skate, then they were allowed to send a skater who did not reach the free segment at World Championships to the Nebelhorn Trophy to qualify the remaining spot(s). Unlike at the World Championships, where countries could qualify more than one spot depending on the placement of their skater(s), at the Nebelhorn Trophy, countries could earn only one spot per discipline, regardless of ranking.[[3]](#cite_note-ISU-2428-3)

Initially, a total of six spots per singles event, three spots in pairs, and four in ice dance were available at Nebelhorn Trophy. One additional quota spot became available in men's singles following the 2021 World Championships.[[4]](#cite_note-:0-4) According to guidelines established by the [International Skating Union](/wiki/International_Skating_Union "International Skating Union") (ISU), nations had to select skaters and teams who have achieved a minimum [total elements score](/wiki/ISU_Judging_System#Total_Elements_Score_(TES) "ISU Judging System") at an ISU-recognized international competition on or before 24 January 2022.[[5]](#cite_note-Qualification-5)

For the team event, scores from the 2021 World Figure Skating Championships and the [2021–22 Grand Prix of Figure Skating](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_ISU_Grand_Prix_of_Figure_Skating "2021–22 ISU Grand Prix of Figure Skating") season were tabulated to establish the top ten nations. Each nation compiled a score from their top performers in each of the four disciplines.[[6]](#cite_note-ISU-2443-6) The [2021 Grand Prix of Figure Skating Final](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_Grand_Prix_of_Figure_Skating_Final "2021–22 Grand Prix of Figure Skating Final"), which was to be held in December 2021,[[5]](#cite_note-Qualification-5) was to be the final event to affect the team event score, but it was cancelled.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

### Men's singles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Figure_skating_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Qualification&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Men's singles")]

Twenty-three quota spots in the men's event were awarded based on the results at the [2021 World Figure Skating Championships](/wiki/2021_World_Figure_Skating_Championships "2021 World Figure Skating Championships").[[2]](#cite_note-ISU-2388-2) Only twenty-three spots out of a possible twenty-four were awarded, because while [Yan Han](/wiki/Yan_Han_(figure_skater) "Yan Han (figure skater)") and [Jin Boyang](/wiki/Jin_Boyang "Jin Boyang") of China both qualified for the free skate, they only earned enough points to qualify for one Olympic entry.[[4]](#cite_note-:0-4) Therefore, the extra quota spot was made available at the [2021 Nebelhorn Trophy](/wiki/2021_CS_Nebelhorn_Trophy "2021 CS Nebelhorn Trophy") along with the other six slots originally allocated.[[3]](#cite_note-ISU-2428-3)

### Team event

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Figure_skating_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Qualification&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Team event")]

In order for a nation to qualify for the team event, it had to have qualified entrants in at least three of the four disciplines ([men's singles, women's singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), [pair skating](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating"), or [ice dance](/wiki/Ice_dance "Ice dance")). If there were not ten nations that had qualified entrants in all four disciplines, nations with three entrants could use an additional athlete quota to fill their team. These additional athletes were eligible to compete in the team event, but not in the individual Olympic events.[[5]](#cite_note-Qualification-5) The nations which qualified for the team event at the 2022 Winter Olympics are identified with a green check mark (![Green tick](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/03/Green_check.svg/20px-Green_check.svg.png)Y) in the last column.
